notes.summary: Sound is a form of energy that propagates through a medium, like air, water, or solids. It travels at different speeds in various mediums and can be affected by temperature changes.
notes.importance: Understanding sound propagation helps us comprehend how sounds travel and why we hear them differently in various environments.
quick_facts: Speed of sound in air is approximately 343 meters per second.
Different materials have different sound speeds.
Sound can be affected by changes in humidity.

notes.summary: The frequency of a sound wave determines its pitch, with higher frequencies producing higher pitches. Frequency is measured in Hertz (Hz), and human hearing ranges from 20 Hz to 20,000 Hz.
notes.importance: Understanding frequency and pitch helps us comprehend how sounds are perceived by the human ear and why different instruments produce distinct tones.
quick_facts: Human hearing range is 20 Hz to 20,000 Hz.
Instruments with higher frequencies sound brighter.

notes.summary: The wavelength of a sound wave is the distance between two consecutive peaks or troughs. Wavelength is related to frequency and speed, and it affects how sounds are perceived.
notes.importance: Understanding wavelength helps us comprehend how sounds travel and why they are affected by different environments.
quick_facts: Wavelength is inversely proportional to frequency.

notes.summary: The amplitude of a sound wave is the maximum displacement from its equilibrium position. Amplitude affects the loudness and intensity of sounds.
notes.importance: Understanding amplitude helps us comprehend how sounds are perceived by the human ear and why different instruments produce distinct tones.

notes.summary: Resonance occurs when a system vibrates at its natural frequency, amplifying the sound wave. Resonance is important in music and acoustics.
notes.importance: Understanding resonance helps us comprehend how sounds are amplified and why certain instruments produce distinctive tones.
quick_facts: Different materials have different resonant frequencies.

notes.summary: Sound reflection occurs when a sound wave bounces off a surface, creating echoes. Reflection affects how sounds are perceived and why we hear them differently in various environments.
notes.importance: Understanding sound reflection helps us comprehend how sounds travel and why they are affected by different surfaces.
quick_facts: Different surfaces reflect sound waves differently.
Sound reflection is important in acoustics.

notes.summary: The speed of sound is affected by the elasticity of a medium. Materials with high elasticity transmit sound faster.
notes.importance: Understanding the relationship between elasticity and sound speed helps us comprehend how sounds travel and why they are affected by different materials.
quick_facts: Materials with high elasticity transmit sound faster.
Different materials have different elastic properties.
Sound speed is affected by temperature.
